Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Adaptive Protocols via Spectral Quantization
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging curvature-aware precision routing and spectral quantization, we can develop a training-free adaptive protocol that outperforms heuristic methods in terms of computational efficiency and numerical stability, while maintaining deterministic execution pathways.
Related Work: Recent work on spectral latent steering uses low-rank subspace projections for efficient test-time optimization but does not address curvature-aware precision routing or training-free adaptive protocols. Our proposal distinguishes itself by explicitly focusing on these aspects, leveraging spectral quantization theory to bridge foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages spectral quantization to develop a training-free adaptive protocol. The framework prioritizes mathematical transparency, explicit numerical stability analysis, and strict computational overhead bounds, establishing a highly feasible pipeline for exploratory single-GPU validation. By integrating spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, we bridge foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. Our approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints under precision switching. Empirical findings anchor to implicit regularization scaling laws, with formal PAC-Bayes verification, systematic ablation protocols, and multi-seed variance quantification serving as mandatory checkpoints. We validate our approach through rigorous evaluation cycles, treating formal theoretical bounds, curvature transparency, and deterministic numerical guardrails as primary deliverables.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, comparing it to heuristic and full-space methods.
Measure computational efficiency and numerical stability using metrics such as hidden-state perturbation overhead, condition number constraints, and deterministic latency bounds.
Perform spectral quantization analysis to validate the theoretical claims and quantify the advantages of the proposed approach.
Conduct systematic ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on computational efficiency and numerical stability.
Verify the empirical findings through multi-seed variance quantification and formal PAC-Bayes verification.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may introduce additional computational overhead compared to heuristic methods, potentially limiting its applicability in resource-constrained environments.
The spectral quantization analysis may not capture all relevant aspects of the optimization landscape, leading to suboptimal routing decisions.
The deterministic execution pathways may not be compatible with all architectural configurations, requiring further adaptation for broader deployment.
